Title: The jojoba lipid droplet protein LDAP1 facilitates the packaging of wax esters into lipid droplets

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy-dense storage lipid; industrial high-value lipid
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LIPID DROPLET-ASSOCIATED PROTEIN (LDAP) family; amphipathic α-helices
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes efficient partitioning/packaging of wax esters from the ER into cytoplasmic lipid droplets, improves wax ester accumulation and lipid droplet morphology, and partially improves seed germination in transgenic Arabidopsis seeds producing wax esters.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested jojoba LD proteins, ScLDAP1 uniquely alleviated wax-ester-induced ER swelling in N. benthamiana leaves co-expressing wax biosynthetic enzymes. 2. ScLDAP1 increased recovery of wax esters in isolated lipid droplet fractions without increasing total wax ester synthesis in transient assays, indicating a packaging rather than biosynthetic role. 3. In transgenic Arabidopsis seeds co-expressing ScWS and MaFAR, ScLDAP1 restored more uniform lipid droplet morphology, increased wax ester accumulation, and improved germination relative to wax-synthesis-only lines. 4. A C-terminal amphipathic helix (helix 6) and specifically residue L162 were necessary/sufficient for wax-ester selectivity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; confocal/AiryScan microscopy; lipid droplet/microsome fractionation; ESI-MS/MS lipid quantification; chimeric protein analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; stable transgenic Arabidopsis seed expression; germination assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundantly expressed in developing jojoba seeds during wax ester accumulation; enriched in isolated lipid droplet proteome
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and Arabidopsis seeds
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in leaves; seed transgenic expression
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear penalty from ScLDAP1 itself reported; wax ester accumulation without proper packaging causes aberrant lipid structures and poor germination, only partially rescued by ScLDAP1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory packaging gene to stack with wax ester biosynthetic genes to improve heterologous wax ester storage, seed lipid droplet organization, and seed performance in engineered oilseeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScLDAP1 function depends on endogenous LDIP in N. benthamiana. Distinct from Arabidopsis/N. benthamiana LDAP isoforms, ScLDAP1 appears specialized for wax ester packaging rather than synthesis.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: napin promoter in transgenic Arabidopsis seeds (for ScLDAP1); transient expression promoter NA
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior proteomic and transcriptomic analyses of developing jojoba seeds identified ScLDAP1 as enriched in WE-accumulating tissues; accession NA

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LDIP (LDAP-interacting protein), hydrophobic LD biogenesis factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for ScLDAP1-mediated efficient wax ester packaging into lipid droplets; RNAi suppression reverses the corrective effect of ScLDAP1 and restores ER defects.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi suppression of endogenous NbLDIP reduced the ability of ScLDAP1 to correct wax-ester-induced ER defects. 2. This supports LDIP dependence of ScLDAP1-mediated wax ester partitioning into lipid droplets.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression in N. benthamiana; qualitative RT-PCR; confocal microscopy; CFP-fluorescence area quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RNAi suppression during transient wax ester reconstitution assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression compromises proper lipid droplet formation/packaging
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates LDIP sufficiency/compatibility should be considered when engineering wax ester packaging systems.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a required cofactor for ScLDAP1 function, supporting analogy between wax-ester-containing and TAG-containing LD biogenesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage lipid; industrially valuable liquid wax
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Esterifies a fatty alcohol with an acyl-CoA to form a wax ester.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FAR drives wax ester production in heterologous tissues; production causes ER defects when wax esters are not efficiently packaged.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of ScWS and ScFAR was required to produce substantial wax esters in N. benthamiana leaves. 2. Wax ester production correlated with swollen ER structures and neutral lipid accumulation. 3. In Arabidopsis seeds, ScWS with MaFAR enabled wax ester accumulation, which was further improved by ScLDAP1 co-expression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; confocal microscopy; ESI-MS/MS; stable transgenic Arabidopsis seed analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: soybean glycinin promoter (gly) in Arabidopsis seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient leaf expression 4 d post infiltration; stable seed expression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wax ester overaccumulation without proper packaging caused ER disruption, aberrant lipid structures, and reduced germination
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core biosynthetic gene for engineering wax ester production; should be stacked with packaging factors such as ScLDAP1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In transient assays, total wax ester amount was not increased by ScLDAP1, indicating ScWS-driven synthesis is separable from packaging efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in leaves and seeds

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage lipid; industrially valuable liquid wax
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces fatty acyl-CoA to fatty alcohol, supplying substrate for wax synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides fatty alcohol precursor required with ScWS for wax ester synthesis in N. benthamiana leaves; contributes to ER defects via wax ester overproduction when packaging is inadequate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScFAR co-expression with ScWS was necessary for wax ester accumulation in N. benthamiana leaves. 2. Expression alone did not trigger wax ester accumulation or ER defects, but together with ScWS caused wax-ester-associated ER swelling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; fluorescent protein localization; ESI-MS/MS; microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to deleterious wax ester accumulation phenotypes if packaging is insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential precursor-supplying gene for wax ester engineering; should be paired with WS and packaging factors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Arabidopsis stable lines, MaFAR rather than ScFAR was used because of broader activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain fatty acid elongation supporting wax ester biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates endogenous fatty acids to very-long-chain fatty acids to better mimic jojoba wax ester substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Modestly increased availability of very-long-chain substrates for wax ester biosynthesis and was included to support optimal wax ester synthesis in subsequent assays.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inclusion of ScFAE caused a modest, not statistically significant, increase in total wax esters in N. benthamiana leaves. 2. It was used subsequently to support elongation of endogenous fatty acids to 20:0 and 22:0 for more jojoba-like substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; ESI-MS/MS lipid quantification; wax species analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supportive precursor-supply gene when reconstructing jojoba-like wax ester production in heterologous plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in subsequent assays for substrate optimization rather than as the main determinant of packaging.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage lipid; industrially valuable liquid wax
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces fatty acyl substrates to fatty alcohols for subsequent esterification into wax esters.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: With ScWS enabled wax ester accumulation in transgenic Arabidopsis seeds; lines showed disrupted lipid packaging and poor germination unless ScLDAP1 was co-expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Used in Arabidopsis because of broader FAR activity than ScFAR. 2. Co-expression with ScWS produced wax esters in seeds and associated lipid packaging defects that were ameliorated by ScLDAP1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic Arabidopsis seed expression; microscopy; WE/TAG quantification; germination assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: soybean β-conglycinin promoter (βcon) in Arabidopsis seeds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wax ester production caused poor germination and aberrant lipid morphology when expressed without ScLDAP1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Broad-activity FAR useful for wax ester engineering in seeds; should be combined with packaging factors.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: YFP:myc-tagged MaFAR entry vector used in construct assembly.
